feed_role: Lab = generator/feed. The clinic SM pipeline (cockpit) owns GATE-1 dedupe, floor-locked rendering, the single preview, and publishing.
note: One creative reused across Instagram, Facebook & Google Business Profile. 10 posts to choose from, diversified by format/hook/topic, each modelled on a niche over-performer and rewritten in EHN clinician voice. Each post carries a routing block mapping it to the pipeline's pillar→format schedule.

pillar_format_map.conversion · Sun: text-led CTA carousel (booking/assessment link in the caption, not on a card)

palette.note: EHN palette only — never the competitor colours/fonts.

typography.note: NOT Libre Caslon — that is the branded-card lock, benched for this experiment.

render_constraints.rule: Shorten copy, never shrink font. The renderer hard-asserts >=36px and crashes the build on an under-floor size.
render_constraints.card_branding: Cards carry the business name "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green close line) — NOT the suburb. Suburb keyword lives in the GBP caption only (local SEO).
render_constraints.gbp: Every post ships a GBP hook card, including text-only days (Zernio silently schedules a card-less GBP post — always supply one).

posts.visual_recipe: Solid sage-green background (4a7c59) with white serif typography. Main headline in large italic serif (~48-56pt): "Why Calcium-D-Glucarate is a Game-Changer for Histamine Intolerance." Subheading in smaller regular serif (~18-22pt) underneath. Minimal spacing, centered layout. Small slide counter (1/7) in dark pill badge top-right. Brand handle (@stevenjudgenaturopath) bottom-left in small sans-serif. No imagery, faces, or illustrations—pure text hierarchy on solid colour.
posts.angle: Oestrogen dominance and histamine intolerance are often two sides of the same coin—here's why they loop together, and what shifts the pattern.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: square carousel (4 slides, 1080 × 1080px each).

Background: soft cream (#F5F3F0) across all slides to harmonize warmly with EHN green #39B54A and avoid pure white sterility. Pairs naturally with sage and charcoal accents.

Headline treatment (Satoshi Bold, ~95–110px, centred):
- Slide 1: "Oestrogen & Histamine" with the word "Histamine" in EHN green #39B54A italic.
- Each subsequent slide opens with a short phrase (e.g., "The Loop Explained," "What Shifts It") in the same bold scale, one accent word in green italic.
- Position headlines in upper third of card, with breathing room above and below.

Subheading (Satoshi Medium, ~40–45px, charcoal #333, centred):
- A short supporting line beneath each headline (e.g., "Why perimenopause stalls DAO and traps histamine").
- Maintains clean whitespace and visual hierarchy matching the winner's minimalist density.

Visual anchor: A thin vertical line in EHN green (#39B54A) sits left-centre of each slide, running from top margin to mid-card—this echoes the winner's solid-colour background technique but renders it as a subtle accent stripe, drawing the eye without overwhelming.

Body copy (Satoshi Medium, ~40px, charcoal): arranged as short bullet or numbered points, one per line, left-aligned to the right of the green stripe (or centred below headline on slides that are text-only). Keep each line to 1–2 short sentences. No line of text shall render below 36px.

Slide counter (1/4) in small dark pill badge (charcoal, 12–14px sans-serif) top-right corner.

Brand lockup (small EHN logo + "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in charcoal, ~14px) bottom-left, unobtrusive.

Composition: minimal, clinical, editorial. No imagery, no faces, no illustrated flourishes—pure typographic hierarchy on cream, accented by green stripe and charcoal text. Margin/padding: 80px left/right, 60px top/bottom on a 1080 canvas, ensuring legible breathing room. The overall feel is grounded, warm, and clinician-credible—never wellness-y or stock.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: square carousel format (multiple slides, 1080 × 1080px each).

Background: soft, warm neutral (cream or off-white) that harmonizes with EHN green #39B54A and supports charcoal and sage accents—never pure white, never pure black, never the competitor's exact palette.

Visual anchor: thin vertical stripe or accent line in EHN green (#39B54A) positioned prominently in the composition (e.g., left-centre, running from top to mid-card, or as a decorative underline). This green must feel intentional and central to the layout, not an afterthought. It replaces the competitor's solid-colour background with a more refined, brand-forward accent.

Typography hierarchy (all Satoshi):
- Headline: Satoshi Bold (~95–110px), centred, with ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic for visual emphasis. Positioned in upper third of card with breathing room.
- Subheading: Satoshi Medium (~40–45px), charcoal, supporting the headline with a single short phrase.
- Body copy: Satoshi Medium (~40px minimum), charcoal, arranged as short points or statements. No text shall render below 36px; shorten copy rather than reducing font size.

Composition: minimal, text-driven, clinical. No imagery, faces, or illustrations—pure typographic elegance on a neutral ground. Margins: ~80px left/right, ~60px top/bottom (1080 canvas), ensuring generous whitespace that mirrors the winner's clean, uncluttered feel.

Slide structure (if carousel):
- Slide counter in small dark pill badge (charcoal, ~12–14px) top-right.
- Brand lockup (small logo + "Elemental Health & Nutrition") bottom-left, ~14px, unobtrusive.

Tone & feel: grounded, editorial, clinician-credible. The green anchor, soft background, and charcoal text create a cohesive, intentional palette that feels warm and trustworthy—never welfare-y, never stock-wellness, never hypey. The layout mirrors the winner's authority-register text hierarchy and minimal visual density, reframed entirely in EHN's colour language.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1: "Oestrogen & Histamine" (Histamine in green italic) | "Why perimenopause often stalls DAO"

Slide 2: "The Loop Explained" (Loop in green italic) | "Excess oestrogen → mast cells release histamine" | "More histamine → less DAO enzyme" | "Worsened intolerance symptoms"

Slide 3: "What You Might Notice" (Notice in green italic) | "Flushing, headaches, bloating during luteal phase" | "Brain fog & fatigue when histamine peaks" | "Worse in perimenopause (and some cycles)"

Slide 4: "What Shifts It" (Shifts in green italic) | "Steady oestrogen support matters" | "Address gut inflammation & DAO function" | "Work with a clinician to personalise"

[All slides: small "Elemental Health & Nutrition" brand line bottom-left, slide counter 1/4 top-right, green vertical accent stripe on left-centre of composition.]
posts.caption:
Oestrogen dominance and histamine intolerance often travel together—and perimenopause can amplify both at once.

Here's the loop: excess oestrogen signals mast cells to release histamine. Meanwhile, the enzyme that breaks down histamine (DAO) doesn't keep up. The result? Flushing, headaches, bloating, and brain fog—often worse in the luteal phase.

The good news: this pattern can shift. It starts with steady oestrogen support, addressing any underlying gut inflammation, and giving your DAO enzyme the nutrients it needs. If you're noticing histamine symptoms that worsen with your cycle, a functional-medicine approach can help you connect the dots and regain stability.

Ready to explore what's driving yours? Let's talk.

#HistamineIntolerance #Perimenopause #OestrogenDominance #WomensHealth #FunctionalMedicine
posts.gmb_caption: Oestrogen dominance and histamine intolerance often loop together in perimenopause—flushing, bloating, and brain fog can worsen with your cycle. Our clinicians help you untangle the pattern and restore balance. Book an appointment at Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood today.

posts.angle: How stress and unresolved trauma can trap your nervous system in fear—and what functional medicine offers
posts.build_brief:
Format: Vertical reel/video (9:16, full-screen mobile). 

Talking-head shot: Female or gender-neutral clinician (white or cream linen shirt, calm professional demeanour) filmed from shoulders-up against a soft sage-green or warm cream background that harmonises with EHN green #39B54A. The background should feel grounded and clinical—consider a subtle texture (linen fabric, natural wall finish) or a muted sage-green wall that echoes the brand anchor without overwhelming. Shot should be direct-to-camera, warm, credible.

Text overlay hierarchy (Satoshi throughout):
- PRIMARY HEADLINE (upper third, ~95px+, Satoshi Bold): "Why Do You Stay Stuck In Fear?" — with one meaningful word ("Stuck") rendered in EHN green #39B54A italic or the word "Fear" in green.
- SECONDARY TEXT (lower third, ~60px, Satoshi Medium): "A functional medicine perspective" in charcoal or soft grey, positioned to mimic the winner's lower-third placement.
- SMALL BRAND LOCK (bottom corner, unobtrusive, ~24–32px): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A.

Composition: mirror the winner's whitespace and clean density—plenty of breathing room around text, no clutter. The clinician's shoulders and upper body fill the frame with safe margins; text overlays sit in the clear space (upper and lower thirds, not crowding the face). Colour palette: sage, cream, charcoal, and EHN green #39B54A only—no purple, no competitor blue. The overall feel is calm, authoritative, warm.

Frame duration: hook (0–1s, question on screen), then a 5–7 second monologue (optional voiceover or on-screen speaking), then close with a soft CTA frame.
posts.design_recipe:
Format: Vertical reel/video (9:16, full-screen mobile) — talking-head format.

Reusable template:
- BACKGROUND: soft sage-green, warm cream, or muted charcoal that harmonises with EHN green #39B54A (never pure black, never bright colour). May include subtle texture (linen, natural finish) for warmth and editorial feel.
- SHOT: clinician/practitioner from shoulders-up, direct-to-camera, calm and credible. Safe margins around shoulders.
- TEXT HIERARCHY:
  - PRIMARY HEADLINE (upper third, ~95px+, Satoshi Bold): [question hook] — one meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic or accent colour.
  - SECONDARY TEXT (lower third, ~60px, Satoshi Medium): [supporting phrase or context], neutral charcoal or soft grey.
  - BRAND LOCK (bottom corner, ~24–32px, Satoshi Medium): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A.
- COMPOSITION: whitespace-rich, clean density. Text overlays in upper and lower thirds, well clear of face. No clutter, no competing visuals.
- COLOUR PALETTE: EHN green #39B54A as primary anchor, sage/cream/charcoal as secondary and background. No secondary colours that clash (no purple, no bright red, no neon).
- TONE: clinical-but-warm, authoritative, grounded, Australian-editorial.
- VOICEOVER/SCRIPT BEATS: hook question (0–1s), short monologue or key statement (5–7s), soft CTA or reflection (final frame).
posts.on_image:
Slide/Frame sequence:

**Hook Frame (0–1s):**
"Why Do You Stay **Stuck** In Fear?"
(word "Stuck" in EHN green #39B54A)

Secondary line: "A functional medicine perspective"

**Main Reel (5–7s talking-head):**
Clinician speaks directly to camera:
"Fear lives in the nervous system. When your body stays in fight-or-flight mode—from old stress, trauma, or even gut dysbiosis—your brain keeps sounding the alarm. You're not broken. Your system is protecting you. Functional medicine looks at what's *actually* triggering that stuck state: inflammation, blood-sugar swings, unresolved grief, a depleted nervous system. Once we address the root, fear loses its grip."

**Close Frame:**
"Let's find what's keeping you stuck."
"Elemental Health & Nutrition"
(green footer with booking prompt)
posts.caption:
Fear doesn't live in your head alone—it's written into your nervous system. When stress or unresolved trauma keeps you in fight-or-flight mode, your body stays defended, even when the threat has long passed.

From a functional-medicine lens, we look deeper: Is your gut dysbiotic? Blood sugar dysregulated? Inflammation chronic? Sleep depleted? These physical imbalances can trap your nervous system in fear and perpetuate anxiety.

You're not broken. Your body is doing exactly what it's been taught to do. The good news? We can help it learn something different.

If you've been stuck in a fear loop and conventional advice hasn't shifted it, it might be time to look at the root. Book a consultation and let's explore what's really going on.

#nervousystem #mentalhealth #functionalfood #anxietyrelief #healthyfoundations
posts.gmb_caption: Stuck in fear or chronic anxiety? Functional medicine looks at the root—your gut, inflammation, stress resilience. We help your nervous system find calm again. Book an appointment in Eastwood, Adelaide to explore what's really going on.

posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Single square card (1080 × 1080px, 4:5 aspect for Instagram/Facebook reels).

Background: Soft warm cream (#F5F1ED) or charcoal-grey (#3A3A3A) — choose cream if the image is warm-toned, charcoal if moody. Avoid pure black and the competitor's teal.

Composition (copy winner's whitespace + hierarchy exactly):
– Upper half (60%): Large bold headline text in Satoshi Bold, ~95–110px, positioned left-aligned or centred. ONE meaningful word (e.g., "METHYLATION" or "MISSED") rendered in EHN green #39B54A italic; remainder in charcoal or cream (inverse of background). Keep headline to 6–8 words max; do NOT exceed this space.
– Middle (10–15%): A supporting subline in Satoshi Medium, 48–56px, in a complementary accent colour (sage #8B9D83 or soft teal #7A9B8E) — e.g., "WHAT YOUR ROUTINE TESTS SKIP" — short, punchy.
– Lower right (25–30%): Portrait image of a middle-aged person (diverse, professional, warm presentation — NOT the competitor's exact subject) holding a lab report or supplement bottle, positioned lower right as per winner's layout. Image should feel clinical-editorial, not stock.
– Background texture (optional but recommended): Very subtle sage or cream texture overlay to soften the card and anchor the green palette.
– Brand lockup (lower left, small, ~80px): Elemental Health & Nutrition wordmark in green #39B54A (or charcoal if background is cream).

Colour palette (CRITICAL):
– PRIMARY ANCHOR: EHN green #39B54A (one word in headline, lockup, subtle accent borders or badge).
– SECONDARY: Sage #8B9D83 or soft teal #7A9B8E (supporting line, fine-line accent frames).
– TERTIARY: Charcoal #3A3A3A or cream #F5F1ED (inverse contrast, body text, background).
– NO red, no neon, no purple, no bright accent that clashes.

Type hierarchy:
– Headline: Satoshi Bold 700, ~100px, one word green #39B54A italic.
– Subline: Satoshi Medium 500, ~50px, sage accent or white on charcoal.
– Brand: Satoshi Bold, small, green or charcoal inverse.

On-card text (EXACT copy to appear on graphic):
Headline: "HOW I MISSED A METHYLATION GLITCH" (with "MISSED" in green #39B54A italic, or "METHYLATION" in green).
Subline: "AND WHY ROUTINE LABS DIDN'T CATCH IT"
Brand close (lower left): "Elemental Health & Nutrition"

Overall feel: Clinical authority, warm-toned warmth, grounded editorial style — match the winner's high-contrast, serious-but-human tone, but render it in sage, cream and green instead of teal and red.
posts.design_recipe:
REUSABLE TEMPLATE — Swap specific copy and image per topic.

Canvas: Single square card (1080 × 1080px).

Background: Soft warm cream (#F5F1ED) or charcoal-grey (#3A3A3A), never pure black or EHN green fill. Choose based on image warmth.

Layout (FIXED):
– Upper 60%: Large Satoshi Bold headline (~95–110px), left-aligned or centred. ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic, rest in charcoal or inverse. Max 6–8 words.
– Middle 10–15%: Short subline in Satoshi Medium (~48–56px), sage #8B9D83 or soft teal #7A9B8E accent.

– Lower left 10%: Small Satoshi Bold brand lockup "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A or charcoal (inverse of background).

Colour Palette (LOCKED):
– Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (one headline word, lockup, fine accents).
– Secondary: Sage #8B9D83 or soft teal #7A9B8E (supporting copy, accent borders).
– Tertiary: Charcoal #3A3A3A or cream #F5F1ED (body, background inverse).

Type (LOCKED):
– Headline: Satoshi Bold 700, ~100px, one word in green italic.
– Subline: Satoshi Medium 500, ~50px, accent colour.
– Brand: Satoshi Bold small, green or charcoal.

Placeholders for topic-agnostic reuse:
– [HEADLINE WORD IN GREEN]: One meaningful clinical term or action verb in green italic.
– [HEADLINE REST]: Remaining headline copy, max 6–8 words.
– [SUPPORTING LINE]: Short punchy subline (~8–10 words), accent colour.
– [IMAGE]: Editorial portrait or clinical illustration, lower right, professional tone.
– [BACKGROUND COLOUR]: Cream or charcoal, inverse to image warmth.

This template locks the winner's whitespace, scale, hierarchy and composition while anchoring every post to EHN green and a clinical-warm aesthetic. Topic copy and image swap freely; the structure and palette stay fixed.
posts.on_image:
Headline: "HOW I MISSED A METHYLATION GLITCH" (with "MISSED" in green #39B54A italic)
Subline: "AND WHY ROUTINE LABS DIDN'T CATCH IT"
Brand close: "Elemental Health & Nutrition"
posts.caption:
Many of my clients come in with "normal" pathology results—but underneath, something isn't sitting right. They're tired. Brain fog won't budge. Skin's not improving. Mood dips for no clear reason.

Methylation is one of those silent players. Your body uses it thousands of times a second: making neurotransmitters, clearing toxins, managing inflammation, building DNA. If your methylation pathway is sluggish—often due to genetics, nutrient gaps, or stress—routine pathology won't flag it. You sit "in range."

But over time, a sluggish methylation cycle can compound. Energy drops. Detox slows. Cognitive sharpness dulls.

Functional-medicine testing picks up what standard labs miss. We look at methylation markers, folate status, B12 forms, homocysteine in context—not just as isolated numbers, but as clues to what's *actually* driving your symptoms.

If you've felt "off" despite normal results, it might be worth a closer look. Let's chat about what's really going on.

**Book a discovery call** to explore whether methylation or another functional pathway needs support.

#MethylationHealth #FunctionalMedicine #EnergyRecovery #MissingPiece #PathologyBeyondNormal
posts.gmb_caption: Routine pathology says you're fine, but you're still fatigued and foggy? Methylation glitches often hide in "normal" ranges. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Adelaide, we use functional-medicine testing to catch what standard labs miss. Book an appointment to discover what might be holding you back.

posts.angle: Intermittent fasting myths vs. evidence: what the science really shows about timing, metabolic health, and whether it's right for you
posts.build_brief:
Create a carousel (4 slides) mimicking the winner's newspaper editorial layout—but rendered in EHN's green-anchored palette. 

**Canvas & format:** 4-slide carousel, each slide 1080×1350px (vertical, Instagram-optimised).

**Colour palette:** Anchor with #39B54A (EHN green) as the primary visual element. Use warm cream (off-white, ~#F5F3F0) or soft sage (~#E8EBE6) as the background—never pure white, never the competitor's blue. Add a complementary accent band (soft charcoal ~#3F3F3F or muted teal ~#6B9A8C) to frame key sections and create hierarchy, matching the winner's high-contrast editorial feel.

**Layout & structure:**
- **Slide 1 (hook):** Large, bold Satoshi headline (~95px+) on the cream/sage background. Headline reads "Fasting *myths*" with the word "myths" in EHN green #39B54A italic. Below, a single supporting line (charcoal, ~50px) in Satoshi Medium: "What the science actually says". Right-align a small, professional headshot of an EHN clinician (portrait, warm neutral tones, clinical but approachable—no stock photos) within a subtle circular frame. Green accent band (~40px height) sits mid-card, breaking the layout like the winner's newspaper column divider. Keep whitespace generous; text should not fight the image.

- **Slide 2:** Charcoal/sage accent band at the top (mimicking newspaper masthead). Headline: "Myth 1: *Fasting* slows your metabolism" (one word in green #39B54A italic). Body copy below in Satoshi Medium (charcoal, ≥40px): "Research shows metabolic rate stays stable during short fasts. Your body adapts intelligently." No more than 2 lines. A small, grounded illustration (line-drawn stomach/digestion icon, green outline) sits bottom-right, subtle and editorial.

- **Slide 3:** Repeat accent-band treatment. Headline: "Myth 2: You *can't* exercise fasted" (one word green italic). Body: "Many athletes thrive fasted. Fuel type matters more than timing." Icon: small green dumbbell or movement symbol. Maintain the cream/sage background throughout.

- **Slide 4 (close):** Full-width charcoal accent band top (like a newspaper footer). Headline: "Ready to explore *fasting*?" (one word green italic). Body copy (Satoshi Medium, ≥40px, white text on charcoal): "We'll tailor an approach that fits your body and goals. No one-size-fits-all." CTA: "Book a consultation" (white, bold, underlined). Brand line bottom: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A italic, small lockup (logo left, text right). Whitespace mirrors Slide 1's editorial clarity.

**Typography & hierarchy:**
- Headlines: Satoshi Bold 700, ~95px+ (Slide 1), ~85px (Slides 2–4). One meaningful word per headline in green #39B54A italic or complementary accent (no neon, no clash).
- Body: Satoshi Medium 500, ≥40px charcoal or white (on charcoal). Keep lines short; break copy into short phrases rather than dense paragraphs.
- Accent bands (charcoal or soft teal): Create vertical/horizontal breaks between sections, echoing the winner's newspaper column structure.

**Imagery & graphics:**
- Slide 1: Clinician headshot (warm, professional, no white coat—trust-building but human). Frame it subtly (soft border, no harsh shadows).
- Slides 2–3: Small, line-drawn health/nutrition icons (green outline, Editorial/clinical feel, not cartoonish). Keep them in the corner, never competing with headline.
- Slide 4: No image—focus on CTA and brand.

**Overall feel:** Editorial, high-contrast, newspaper-inspired (like the winner) but rendered entirely in EHN's green-and-neutral palette. Generous whitespace. Clinician authority. No wellness clichés, no stock photography. Warm and trustworthy.
posts.design_recipe:
**Reusable EHN Editorial Carousel Template** (4-slide carousel, 1080×1350px per slide)

**Colour system (always consistent):**
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (highlights one word per headline in italic, used in accent bands, footer branding)
- Background: Warm cream (~#F5F3F0) or soft sage (~#E8EBE6)
- Accent band: Soft charcoal (~#3F3F3F) or muted teal (~#6B9A8C) for hierarchy breaks
- Text: Charcoal or white (on charcoal accent); no pure black
- Never use the competitor's colour palette; never use pure white background or pure black text

**Typography (locked):**
- Headlines: Satoshi Bold 700, ~95px (Slide 1), ~85px (Slides 2–4). ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic.
- Body: Satoshi Medium 500, ≥40px. Short phrases, generous line-height, never cramped.
- Accent band text: Satoshi Bold 700, white on charcoal or charcoal on cream.

**Slide 1 (hook/introduction):**
- Layout: Full-bleed cream/sage background. Generous left/right margins (~60px).
- Headline: Centered top, ~95px Satoshi Bold. Format: "[Word] *[word]*" (second word in green #39B54A italic). Whitespace below.
- Supporting line: Centered below headline, ~50px Satoshi Medium, charcoal, one short phrase.
- Image: Right-aligned or centered portrait (clinician or relevant subject), subtle frame (soft border, no harsh effects), warm neutral tones, ~300×300px.
- Accent band: Horizontal green/charcoal band (~40px height) sits mid-card, creating a visual break (like a newspaper divider).
- Closing whitespace: 20–30% bottom margin.

**Slides 2–3 (supporting points):**
- Layout: Cream/sage background, ~60px margins.
- Accent band top: Charcoal or soft teal, ~40px height, left-aligned.
- Headline: Inside or just below accent band, Satoshi Bold ~85px, charcoal or white (depends on band colour). Format: "[Word] *[Word]*" (one word in green #39B54A italic).
- Body: Below headline, Satoshi Medium ≥40px, charcoal, 1–2 short sentences. Generous line-height.
- Icon: Small (40–60px), bottom-right or side-aligned, green outline, editorial/clinical feel (no cartoons).
- Whitespace: 15–25% bottom margin.

**Slide 4 (close/CTA):**
- Layout: Cream/sage background top 30%, full-bleed charcoal accent band bottom 70%.
- Headline (on cream, top): Satoshi Bold ~85px, charcoal, format "[Word] *[word]*" (one word green italic).
- Body (on charcoal band): Satoshi Medium ≥40px, white, 2–3 sentences maximum. Centered or left-aligned.
- CTA button: "Book a consultation" (or equivalent), white bold text, underlined, centered, mid-band.
- Brand line: Bottom, "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A italic, small lockup (logo + text), right-aligned or centered.
- Whitespace: Balanced top and bottom margins.

**Overall design principles:**
- **Hierarchy:** Headline > body > icon. No competing elements.
- **Whitespace:** Generous margins, never cramped. Mimics editorial newspaper layout.
- **Accent bands:** Charcoal or soft teal horizontal/vertical breaks echo newspaper column structure.
- **Imagery:** Clinician, subject, or lifestyle photography (warm, professional, human-scale; no stock wellness clichés).
- **Colour coherence:** Green always anchors; secondary colours harmonize, never clash. No pure black, no pure white, no competitor's palette.
- **Mood:** Clinical, warm, trustworthy, editorial. Authority without arrogance.

This template grafts onto any topic: swap [headline], [supporting line], [icon], [body points], [CTA]—the layout, colour system, and hierarchy remain constant.
posts.on_image:

posts.caption:
Fasting gets a lot of hype—and a lot of misinformation. The truth? The science is nuanced, and what works depends entirely on *you*.

We see patients thrive with intermittent fasting, and others who feel better with regular meals. Neither is "wrong"—it's about listening to your body and working with evidence, not ideology.

If you're curious whether fasting might support your goals (whether that's better energy, improved digestion, or metabolic health), we're here to explore it properly. No dogma, just personalised functional medicine.

Ready to get the real story? Let's chat.

#IntermittentFasting #FunctionalMedicine #NutritionScience #EvidenceBasedHealth #AdelaideHealthClinic
posts.gmb_caption: Fasting myths vs. science—we separate the hype from the evidence at Elemental Health & Nutrition in Adelaide. Whether intermittent fasting is right for you depends on your unique health picture. Book an appointment to explore a personalised approach.

posts.build_brief:
Format: vertical reel cover (1080×1920 canvas) with talking-head frame shot (mid-shoulder, clinician in clinical/warm office setting, smiling naturally at camera, blurred background for depth).

Layout: Mimic the winner's lower-third text overlay composition. Position a ROUNDED-RECTANGLE TEXT BOX (soft sage or warm cream fill, no border) in the lower third of the frame, horizontally centred, with substantial whitespace above the video frame and below the card edge.

Typography on card:
- Headline: Satoshi Bold, ~95px–110px, reading "Who are you *walking* with today?" (the word "walking" in EHN green #39B54A italic, bold weight)
- Body line below: Satoshi Medium, ~48px–54px, charcoal, reading "Movement opens doors—physical and emotional."
- Small close line: Satoshi Medium, ~36px, charcoal or muted grey, "Elemental Health & Nutrition" centred at card base

Colour palette:
- Primary visual anchor: EHN green #39B54A (used on the one italic word in headline, and as a subtle accent line or dot above/below the card for visual weight)
- Card fill: warm cream or soft sage (very muted, never competitive teal—think Australian natural neutrals)
- Background: cream or off-white video frame, clinical warmth (like a podcast/clinic setting)
- Text: charcoal or near-black for body and close line

Design rhythm:
- Large whitespace above card (clinician face + shoulders in that space)
- Card sits lower-third, self-contained, easy to read at thumb-scroll speed
- One green accent word (walking) + green brand close line = two green moments, anchoring the whole composition
- No drop shadow; soft, clinical edge treatment matching the warmth of the talking-head shot

Vibe: Clinical warmth, approachable, inviting—a functional-medicine clinician speaking directly about the whole-person benefits of movement. No clichés, no overpromising. Grounded, Australian, editorial.
posts.design_recipe:
Format: vertical reel cover (1080×1920 canvas) with talking-head frame (mid-shoulder shot, clinical/warm setting, natural smile, blurred background).

Layout template (reusable):
- Talking-head occupies upper portion (face + shoulders frame, natural lighting, clinical backdrop)
- Lower third: ROUNDED-RECTANGLE TEXT OVERLAY (soft neutral fill—cream, sage, or warm grey, not pure white, never competitor's teal), horizontally centred
- Substantial whitespace between head and card (breathing room for thumb-scroll legibility)

Typography hierarchy (Satoshi throughout):
- Headline: BOLD, ~95–110px, two lines max; ONE MEANINGFUL WORD in EHN green #39B54A italic (bold weight) for visual anchor
- Supporting line: MEDIUM, ~48–54px, charcoal or muted neutral, plain language
- Brand close: MEDIUM, ~36px, charcoal or grey, "Elemental Health & Nutrition" centred

Colour system (always green-anchored):
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A on the one highlighted word in headline + small accent detail (line, dot, or underline) for visual weight distribution
- Card fill: warm, muted neutral (cream, sage, soft grey-blue—never pure white, never competitive palette)
- Video background: cream, white, or soft clinical-natural tone (office/clinic setting feel)
- Text: charcoal, dark grey, or near-black for legibility; never the competitor's colour system

Shape & spacing:
- Rounded rectangle (soft border radius, ~16–24px)
- Card padding: ample internal whitespace around text
- Overall composition: clinical yet warm, minimal clutter, one clear visual hierarchy
- No drop shadow or heavy effects; edge treatment should feel clean and intentional

Tone: Direct address to camera, conversational, evidence-rooted, approachable—a clinician speaking to the viewer about a whole-person health topic. Reusable for movement, nutrition, sleep, stress, gut health, etc.

Graphic asset: Logo lockup (small, unobtrusive, bottom corner or integrated into brand close)
posts.on_image:
Slide/Reel Hook Frame (on-screen text overlay):

Headline: "Who are you *walking* with today?" (with "walking" in EHN green #39B54A, bold italic)

Supporting line: "Movement opens doors—physical and emotional."

Brand close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition"

(Script outline for video beat: Open on clinician, warm smile at camera, 1–2 seconds of natural eye contact; text card fades/slides in lower-third; clinician speaks briefly [optional voiceover] about movement as a bridge to conversation and wellbeing. Keep tone conversational, inviting—"Who do you want to move alongside today?")
posts.caption:
Walking isn't just a step forward—it's an invitation. Whether you're moving solo or sharing the path with someone you care about, walking opens conversations and space for connection in a way sitting down often can't.

Movement helps us think more clearly, regulate our nervous system, and—honestly—it often brings out conversations we've been needing to have. There's something about the rhythm of walking that settles the mind and loosens the tongue.

Who could you invite for a walk this week? Even 10 minutes makes a difference—to your body, your mind, and your relationships.

Book a chat with us about building movement into your everyday routine. We'd love to help you find what works for *your* body.

#WalkingMatters #MovementMedicine #FunctionalHealth #WellnessAdelaide #ClinicianAdvice
posts.gmb_caption: Walking is one of the most underrated tools for whole-person health. In Eastwood, Adelaide, we see how movement—especially walking—opens conversations and boosts both physical and mental wellbeing. Ready to discover how to build movement into your life? Book an appointment with our team.

posts.visual_recipe: Clean, minimal layout on a soft off-white background. Circular tan/beige profile photo (headshot of male authority figure) positioned top-left, adjacent to bold sans-serif text in black. Two short text blocks: first lists 5 joy-related practices with their biological effects in listicle style (~24 words), second is a standalone thesis statement ("Joy is a biological necessity") in larger, emphasis font (~7 words). No additional imagery, overlays, or visual noise—pure typographic hierarchy with authoritative credibility signal (verified profile, M.D. credential).
posts.angle: Sleep quality is not a luxury—it's a biological pillar of mood, immunity, and mental clarity.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Single square card (1080 × 1080px), soft cream background (#F5F1ED or similar warm neutral).

Layout structure: Replicate the winner's clean, minimal typographic hierarchy with generous whitespace.

Top-left (above headline): Small, circular EHN logo or initials in green #39B54A, roughly 60px diameter, positioned 40px from top-left corner.

Headline placement: Large, bold sans-serif (Satoshi Bold, ~95–110px scale on 1080 canvas, ~7–8 words max). Position 50px left margin, starting ~120px from top. The word "sleep" rendered in green #39B54A italic; remainder in charcoal (#2C2C2C). Example: "Sleep shapes [green]your[/green] mind."

First text block (listicle): ~120px below headline, left-aligned, Satoshi Medium, charcoal. Five short practices (deep sleep supports emotional regulation, REM dreaming consolidates memory, slow-wave sleep clears brain toxins, melatonin stabilises mood, quality rest strengthens resilience). Format as a compact bulleted or line-break list, ~50–60px line height, keeping total block ~200–250px tall.

Second text block (thesis): ~80px below listicle, left-aligned, Satoshi Bold, charcoal, ~60–70px scale, ~5–7 words: "Sleep is a biological necessity." Left margin 40–50px.

Bottom brand line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A, Satoshi Medium, ~32–36px, bottom-left corner, 30px from edges.

Secondary palette: Soft cream + charcoal + green #39B54A. No additional imagery, borders, or overlays. Pure typographic authority, warm and clinical.

Whitespace: Mimic the winner's airy ratio—roughly 30% white space, 70% text and brand elements. Breathing room between blocks is essential.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: Single square card (1080 × 1080px), soft neutral background (cream, warm grey, or muted sage) that harmonizes with EHN green #39B54A—never pure white, never the competitor's exact palette.

Top-left anchor: Small circular logo or initial mark in green #39B54A, ~60px diameter, positioned 40px from top-left.

Headline tier: Large, bold Satoshi (700 weight, ~95–110px scale), positioned left-aligned at ~120px from top, ~50px left margin. ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic; remainder in charcoal (#2C2C2C). Cap headline to ~7–8 words so no line-break forces text below 36px.

Primary content block (listicle/short points): Positioned ~120px below headline, left-aligned, Satoshi Medium (500), charcoal. Three to five short statements (biological benefits or clinically-grounded points). Format as compact line-break list or subtle bullets, ~50–60px line height. Total block height ~200–250px. This is your evidence anchor—plain-language, non-hypey, credible.

Secondary statement block (thesis): Positioned ~80px below primary block, left-aligned, Satoshi Bold (700), charcoal, ~60–70px scale. Single statement, ~5–7 words. This is your authority punch—it echoes the headline but lands differently.

Bottom brand line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A, Satoshi Medium (500), ~32–36px, bottom-left corner, 30px clearance from edges.

Palette: EHN green #39B54A as the visual anchor (headline word + logo + brand line). Secondary palette (background + body text) chosen to harmonize—sage, cream, soft grey-blue, charcoal, warm neutrals. Avoid pure black background, pure white background, or any colour that clashes with green (no purple, bright pink, orange, strong red, neon).

Composition: Minimal, editorial, no overlays, no imagery, no borders. Pure typographic hierarchy with clinical warmth. Whitespace ratio ~30% air, 70% content—breathing room between text blocks is non-negotiable. Every element serves credibility and readability.
posts.on_image: Headline: Sleep shapes your mind. | First block: Deep sleep supports emotional regulation. REM dreaming consolidates memory. Slow-wave sleep clears brain toxins. Melatonin stabilises mood. Quality rest strengthens resilience. | Second block: Sleep is a biological necessity. | Brand line: Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Sleep is not a luxury—it's a biological load-bearing pillar. When you sleep well, your brain clears metabolic waste, consolidates memory, regulates mood, and rebuilds resilience. When you don't, everything frays: focus dims, mood plummets, immunity softens, and anxiety creeps in.

Deep sleep supports emotional regulation. REM dreaming locks in learning. Slow-wave sleep is when your brain does its housekeeping. Melatonin isn't just about shutting your eyes—it stabilises mood, reduces inflammation, and protects your nervous system.

If you're struggling with sleep or anxiety, poor rest is almost always part of the story. Let's talk about what's really happening—and what to rebuild. 

Ready to explore what's driving your sleep? Book a consultation with us.

#SleepAndMentalHealth #FunctionalMedicine #MentalWellness #AustralianNaturopath #ClinicalNutrition
posts.gmb_caption: Sleep quality shapes your mood, immunity, and mental clarity. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Adelaide, we investigate what's blocking your rest and rebuild resilience from the ground up. If poor sleep or anxiety is affecting your life, let's work out what's really happening. Book an appointment with us today.

posts.visual_recipe: Text-only Facebook post with no image available. Opening hook uses contrarian stat ("Your vet probably isn't telling you this") followed by alarming health metric (3 in 4 dogs get cancer). Body structured as 4 bullet-point revelations each debunking conventional pet-care wisdom (kibble ingredients, spaying age, neurotoxin flea meds, vaccine boosters). Authority established through credentials (Dr. Judy Morgan: 40 years, 9 books). Closes with podcast CTA link. Emphasis on hidden industry truths and regulatory failures; designed to trigger concern and drive engagement to full episode.
posts.angle: Hidden mould & microplastics burden — what functional medicine reveals about everyday exposure
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Text-only Facebook card, vertical orientation (1080 × 1350px). Background: soft cream (#F5F2ED) to complement and soften EHN green #39B54A. No image.

Layout & hierarchy (replicating winner's structure):
• Opening hook (top, bold impact): Single contrarian stat in LARGE bold Satoshi, one word highlighted in green #39B54A italic. Example placement: "Your GP probably isn't testing you for this. 1 in 3 Australian homes have hidden mould."
• Generous whitespace below hook (breath, visual pause).
• Body: 4 short bullet-point revelations, each 1–2 lines max, charcoal text (Satoshi Medium, hierarchically smaller than headline but readable at ≥40px). Each bullet debunks a common assumption (e.g., "You can't see mould — it hides behind walls" | "Microplastics in water bottles accumulate faster than you think" | "Mould mycotoxins trigger inflammation without obvious symptoms" | "Standard cleaning won't kill deep spore colonies").
• Authority line: 1–2 sentences, charcoal, smaller hierarchy, establishes clinical credibility (e.g., "Dr [EHN Practitioner]: [years] experience, functional medicine specialist" or "Elemental Health & Nutrition: evidence-based toxin assessment").
• Generous whitespace before close.
• Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A, Satoshi Medium, centred, 60–80px. Minimal ornament (small logo if available, left-aligned or centred below text).
• Soft CTA: single line, sage or muted teal accent, "Book a toxin assessment" or "Learn your exposure risk — consult our team".
• Overall feel: clinical, grounded, calm (not alarming). Cream + green + charcoal. Ample whitespace mirrors premium healthcare comms, not clickbait density.
posts.design_recipe:
REUSABLE TEMPLATE (topic-agnostic):

Canvas: Text-only vertical card (1080 × 1350px). Background: Soft, neutral (cream, warm white, or pale sage) chosen to harmonize with and showcase EHN green #39B54A as the primary visual anchor.

Colour palette (locked across all iterations of this template):
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A
- Secondary: sage, teal, or muted blue-grey (for accent lines, CTAs)
- Neutrals: charcoal (#2C2C2C) for body text, cream/off-white for background
- NEVER: pure black, pure white alone, clashing hues (purple, bright pink, orange, neon)

Typography lock:
- Headline: Satoshi Bold (700), ~95–110px equivalent on 1080 canvas, charcoal or dark grey, ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic
- Authority/credential line: Satoshi Medium (500), ~48–56px, charcoal
- Body bullets: Satoshi Medium (500), ≥40px, charcoal, 4 short lines (max 1–2 lines each)
- Brand/close line: Satoshi Medium, 60–80px, EHN green #39B54A, centred
- CTA line: Satoshi Medium, 36–44px, sage or muted teal accent

Composition & whitespace:
- Top margin: substantial (120–160px), establishes breathing room
- Headline block: 1 contrarian stat + 1 supporting metric, bold impact
- Mid-card whitespace: 80–120px (visual pause after hook)
- Bullet block: 4 short revelations, each separated by 20–40px of whitespace
- Authority line: 40–60px below bullets
- Bottom whitespace: 60–100px before brand line
- Brand line + logo: 40–60px above footer
- CTA line: minimal footer, 30–40px clearance below

Layout rules:
- NO images, NO flourishes, NO borders (unless a single thin green accent rule above CTA)
- Centred or left-aligned text (test both; centre tends to feel premium)
- Generous padding on left/right (80–120px from canvas edge)
- Every element breathes; density ≤50% of canvas
- Green #39B54A appears as: (a) one word highlight in headline, (b) brand/close line, (c) optional thin accent rule or dot separator

Overall feel: clinical-calm, editorial, grounded, premium. Colour-anchored to green but never overwhelming. Whitespace-forward. Typographic hierarchy is the primary visual device, not imagery or pattern.
posts.on_image:

posts.caption: Your GP probably isn't testing you for this. One in three Australian homes harbour hidden mould, and most of us are exposed to microplastics every day — yet routine testing rarely picks them up. At Elemental Health & Nutrition, we use functional medicine to reveal what's hiding in your environment and your body. Mould mycotoxins and microplastics can trigger chronic inflammation, brain fog and fatigue years before symptoms become obvious. This isn't about fear — it's about evidence. We assess your toxin exposure, guide you toward safer choices, and support your body's natural ability to clear what it's been carrying. Ready to find out what you're actually exposed to? Book a consultation and let's get you tested. #FunctionalMedicine #MouldTesting #ToxinExposure #MicroplasticAwareness #AdelaideWellness
posts.gmb_caption: Did you know one in three Australian homes have hidden mould? Microplastics and mycotoxins can trigger chronic inflammation without obvious symptoms — and most GPs don't test for them. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Adelaide, we use functional medicine to reveal your real toxin exposure and help your body clear what's been hiding. Book a toxin assessment today.

routing.gate1_note: Topic "toxins/mould/microplastics" ran in EHN's last ~4 weeks — pipeline GATE-1 will likely collapse/drop this pick.

posts.visual_recipe: Professional headshot of a smiling 60+ man in formal business attire (dark suit, grey tie) positioned center-frame in an official parliamentary/boardroom setting with tan leather chairs blurred in background. White serif text overlaid in two stacked left-aligned blocks: "I just turned 66." (larger, bolder) above "Here are 5 things I wish I knew at 26..." (slightly smaller, with "at 26" italicized). Soft warm lighting on subject, sharp focus on face with blurred formal institutional background to convey authority and credibility.
posts.angle: Reframe longevity as health choices you can start making today, whatever your age — drawing on lived wisdom and functional medicine principles.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Carousel (3–4 slides, vertical mobile-first, 1080×1350 per slide).

Slide 1 (Hook):
- Format: Full-bleed photograph of a vibrant, healthy 50+ woman in natural light, positioned centre-frame, wearing neutral tones (cream, soft grey). Sharp focus on her face and shoulders; warm, slightly diffused background (a clinical but welcoming interior — think modern wellness space, not boardroom — soft cream or sage wall blurred behind).
- Text overlay in Satoshi Bold: headline "I'm 55." (approx. 95px, white/cream text, left-aligned, upper-left quadrant). Below it, subline in Satoshi Medium: "Here are 5 things I wish I'd known at 25..." (approx. 60px, white/cream text, left-aligned, lower-left quadrant). One meaningful word ("I'd") in green #39B54A italic for visual anchor.
- Whitespace: Generous left margin (20–25% of canvas); text floats freely over image without background box. This echoes the competitor's official-headshot-with-overlay structure but swaps institutional boardroom for warm, clinical wellness aesthetic.
- Overall feel: Warm, accessible, credible — approachable expertise, not corporate authority.

Slide 2 (Point 1):
- Background: Soft warm cream (off-white, #F5F2ED or similar — harmonizes with green #39B54A without competing).
- Left third: Small illustration or icon in green #39B54A (e.g., a stylised leaf or heart icon, grounded editorial style, ~120px). Plenty of whitespace around it.
- Right two-thirds: Satoshi Bold headline "Prevention" (approx. 80px, green #39B54A, one word). Below: Satoshi Medium body copy (approx. 48px, charcoal #2C2C2C): "beats treatment every time." 
- Subline (approx. 40px, charcoal, Satoshi Medium): "Start now, feel the difference in 12 weeks."
- Hierarchy: Icon on left anchors the eye; headline in green; body plain, credible. No background box; text sits cleanly on cream.

Slide 3 (Point 2):
- Background: Sage green or soft teal-grey (complementary to #39B54A, e.g., #A8C5A8 or #8FA9A8), muted and warm.
- Right third: Small icon or illustration in cream/white (inverted from Slide 2, visual variety while maintaining hierarchy).
- Left two-thirds: Satoshi Bold headline "Sleep" (approx. 80px, cream/white, one word). Below: Satoshi Medium body copy (approx. 48px, cream/white): "is not a luxury — it's medicine."
- Subline (approx. 40px, cream/white, Satoshi Medium): "Prioritise it like you would a prescription."
- Background swap (sage/teal instead of cream) creates visual rhythm without losing the green anchor. Icon and text are inverted in position from Slide 2, keeping carousel dynamic.

Slide 4 (Call-to-action):
- Background: Cream again (#F5F2ED), echoing Slide 2 for symmetry.
- Centred composition: Large Satoshi Bold headline "Your age is" (approx. 85px, charcoal) with one word "negotiable" in green #39B54A italic (approx. 85px, same line or stacked).
- Subline (approx. 48px, Satoshi Medium, charcoal): "Your health is entirely in your control."
- Footer (approx. 44px, Satoshi Medium, green #39B54A): "Let's talk about what's possible for you."
- Brand lockup: Small "Elemental Health & Nutrition" logo/text in green #39B54A at the very bottom (approx. 32px, unobtrusive). 
- Whitespace: Generous top/bottom/side margins; text floats centred, not crammed.

Overall palette: Cream (#F5F2ED) + sage/soft teal (#A8C5A8 or #8FA9A8) + charcoal (#2C2C2C) + green #39B54A as the primary visual anchor. Never pure black; never the competitor's tan/grey/white institutional palette. The complementary secondary colours (sage/soft teal) harmonize with green, evoking warmth and natural health without clashing. This feels clinical, warm, and grounded—exactly EHN's voice.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: Carousel template (3–4 slides, 1080×1350 vertical, mobile-first).

Slide 1 (Hero Hook) — Reusable structure:
- Full-bleed photograph (centre-frame subject, warm natural or clinical-wellness lighting) with soft, blurred neutral background (cream or sage-toned interior space).
- Text overlay: Satoshi Bold headline (approx. 95px, cream/white text, left-aligned, upper-left quadrant, ~8 words max) + Satoshi Medium subline (approx. 60px, cream/white, left-aligned, lower-left quadrant). ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic.
- Generous left whitespace margin (20–25% canvas width); text floats freely over image, no background box.
- Overall feel: Warm, accessible, credible expertise.

Slides 2–3 (Points/Rhythm) — Alternating layout:
- Background alternates: Slide 2 = Cream (#F5F2ED); Slide 3 = Sage/soft teal complement (e.g., #A8C5A8). Swap back to cream for any additional point slides.
- One-third holds icon/illustration in [primary colour] (green #39B54A for cream slides; cream/white for sage slides), ~120px, grounded editorial style, generous whitespace.
- Two-thirds holds text: Satoshi Bold headline [~80px, matching one-word theme], then Satoshi Medium body [~48px], then Satoshi Medium subline [~40px, slightly smaller]. All text in readable neutral (charcoal #2C2C2C for cream; cream/white for sage).
- Icon position flips left-right between slides 2 and 3 (visual variety, consistent structure).
- No background boxes; text sits clean on coloured surface. Hard legibility floor: all text >=36px (body >=40px, headline ~95px on slide 1, ~80px on point slides).

Slide 4 (CTA/Close) — Centred composition:
- Background: Cream (#F5F2ED), echoing Slide 2 for symmetry.
- Centred text: Satoshi Bold main headline (approx. 85px, charcoal), with one meaningful word in green #39B54A italic (approx. 85px, same scale).
- Below: Satoshi Medium subline (approx. 48px, charcoal), then Satoshi Medium footer/CTA (approx. 44px, green #39B54A).
- Brand lockup: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (approx. 32px, Satoshi, green #39B54A) at foot.
- Generous top/bottom/side margins; centred composition, not cramped.

Colour palette (LOCKED, reusable):
- Primary anchor: Green #39B54A (headlines, icons on cream slides, footer/CTA text).
- Secondary backgrounds: Cream (#F5F2ED) + Sage/soft teal (e.g., #A8C5A8 or #8FA9A8). These harmonize with green, never clash.
- Text: Charcoal #2C2C2C on cream; cream/white on sage. Never pure black; never the competitor's palette.
- Feel: Clinical, warm, grounded, editorial — Australian-natural (sage, cream, charcoal, green). No stock-wellness clichés, no neon, no bright pops.

Typography (LOCKED):
- Headlines: Satoshi Bold (700).
- Body/sublines: Satoshi Medium (500).
- One meaningful word per headline in green #39B54A italic or complementary accent colour for visual punctuation.
- All text >=36px; body >=40px; slide 1 headline ~95px; point-slide headlines ~80px; CTA ~85px. SHORTEN COPY, NEVER SHRINK FONT.

Brand lock (LOCKED):
- Card closes with "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (not suburb) in green #39B54A, small and unobtrusive at footer.
- Booking/CTA destination in footer or Slide 4.
- AHPRA/TGA-safe: no doses, no cure/treat/guarantee/miracle claims, no before-after imagery.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1: "I'm 55." | "Here are 5 things I wish I'd known at 25..."

Slide 2: "Prevention" | "beats treatment every time." | "Start now, feel the difference in 12 weeks."

Slide 3: "Sleep" | "is not a luxury — it's medicine." | "Prioritise it like you would a prescription."

Slide 4: "Your age is" [with "negotiable" in green italic] | "Your health is entirely in your control." | "Let's talk about what's possible for you." | "Elemental Health & Nutrition"
posts.caption:
I'm 55, and I've learned something crucial: the choices you make now compound over decades. 

These five lessons took me 30 years to understand, but you don't have to wait. Prevention, sleep, movement, nutrition, stress resilience — they all sit in your control. And the best time to start isn't when you're in crisis; it's today.

Your age isn't fixed. Your health isn't luck. At Elemental Health & Nutrition, we use functional medicine to help you understand what your body needs to thrive, not just survive.

What's one habit shift you'd make if you knew it would ripple forward 30 years?

#LongevityMedicine #FunctionalHealth #PreventiveCare #AgingWell #AdelaideHealth
posts.gmb_caption: At 55, I've learned that longevity isn't luck — it's the sum of choices made today. If you're ready to invest in your health now and reap the benefits for decades, let's talk. Our functional medicine approach focuses on prevention, not just treatment. Book an appointment in Eastwood, Adelaide, and let's explore what's possible for you.

posts.visual_recipe: A talking-head video still of a bald male professional (psychiatrist/doctor) in a white shirt against a blue gradient background, holding a pink brain model. Bold sans-serif text overlay in white reads "Is It Borderline Personality Disorder, ADHD, or Bipolar? (Psychiatrist Answers)" positioned prominently above chest level. Subtitle text below. The framing is medium close-up, conversational and direct—designed to trigger pattern-matching curiosity around diagnosis differentiation.
posts.angle: Gut dysbiosis, ADHD, or anxiety? How functional medicine differentiates the overlap and why it matters for your care.
posts.build_brief: Create a VIDEO REEL STILL (format: 1080×1920 vertical reel cover) featuring a warm, approachable female clinician (nutritionist or functional-medicine practitioner) in a sage or cream clinical gown against a soft warm-cream gradient background (never blue, never the competitor's palette). She holds a clear glass digestive-system model or diagram illustration in her hands at chest level, positioned naturally. HEADLINE text overlay in Satoshi Bold (~95px+) reads "Gut Problems, ADHD, or Anxiety? (Functional Medicine Explains)" — position prominently above chest level, white text with ONE meaningful word ("Gut" or "Anxiety") highlighted in EHN green #39B54A italic. Subtitle text (Satoshi Medium, ~40px+) sits below the headline: a short supporting line like "What your GP might miss." Ensure generous white space mirrors the winner's conversational close-up framing — medium close-up, direct eye contact if possible, clinical but warm. Green #39B54A acts as the visual anchor via the ONE highlighted word in the headline and subtle accent lines or a thin green border frame. Secondary palette: warm cream background, sage undertones, charcoal for any secondary text. The overall feel is grounded, editorial, Australian-natural — a clinician you'd trust, holding a tangible teaching tool. No pure black. No competitor's exact blue. Never a full green background. Legibility lock: headline and subtitle sit at least 36px+, headline closer to 95px+ to match the winner's scale and impact. The design should feel like a curiosity-driven diagnosis-differentiation reel in EHN's colour world.
posts.design_recipe: REUSABLE REEL TEMPLATE — Vertical video still (1080×1920), question-bait hook format. CANVAS: soft warm-cream gradient background (never pure black, never a full solid colour). COMPOSITION: medium close-up framing of a clinician/expert in natural light, holding a tangible model or tool at chest level. TYPOGRAPHY: Headline in Satoshi Bold (~95px+), with ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic to act as visual anchor and pattern-match trigger; supporting subline in Satoshi Medium (~40px+) positioned directly below headline, charcoal or warm neutral text, short and punchy. COLOUR PALETTE: EHN green #39B54A as the ONLY bright accent (one word only in headline + optional thin accent lines); secondary palette of sage, warm cream, soft grey-brown, charcoal — all colours chosen to harmonize with green and never clash (no purple, pink, orange, neon). WHITESPACE: generous margins mirror the winner's conversational density — text sits high on the frame, leaving breathing room for the clinician and tool. VISUAL ELEMENTS: one grounded, editorial prop (model, diagram, tool) held naturally; optional subtle decorative lines in green #39B54A to frame or anchor. BRAND: small unobtrusive logo lockup in corner; "Elemental Health & Nutrition" as close line (never the suburb). LEGIBILITY LOCK: all text >=36px (headline 95px+, body 40px+). Copy shortened to fit hierarchy, never shrinking font. The reel should feel like a clinical curiosity-trigger — "what am I missing?" — rendered in warm, accessible EHN green-anchored tones.
posts.on_image: Slide 1 (Hook Frame – on-screen text): "Gut Problems, ADHD, or Anxiety? | (Functional Medicine Explains)" — [highlight "Gut" or "Anxiety" in green #39B54A italic] | Supporting line: "What your GP might miss"
posts.caption:
Many people are told they have ADHD or anxiety when the real culprit is something entirely different—like gut dysbiosis, nutrient deficiency or metabolic imbalance.

At Elemental Health & Nutrition, we dig deeper. We don't just manage labels; we investigate *why* symptoms show up in the first place. That's functional medicine—pattern-matching the root cause, not just the name.

If you've tried the usual treatments and still feel stuck, it might be time for a different lens. We're here to help you understand what's actually going on.

Ready to get to the root? Book a consultation with us.

#FunctionalMedicine #GutHealth #ADHD #MentalHealthMatters #AustralianHealth
posts.gmb_caption: If gut problems, ADHD or anxiety diagnoses haven't worked out the way you'd hoped, functional medicine might offer answers. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we investigate the root cause—not just treat the label. Book an appointment to explore what's really going on.

posts.visual_recipe: Photo of a person in a dark bathroom shower lit by warm orange candlelight from below, creating dramatic low-light ambiance. White serif bold text (approx 28 words) overlaid center-frame reads "You were today years old when you found out you should be taking dark showers every night. Here's why.." Text uses semi-transparent dark overlay for contrast against the warm-lit bathroom scene. Candlelight and dim bathroom fixtures create the primary visual narrative of calm, parasympathetic-nervous-system activation.
posts.angle: Dark showers activate parasympathetic calm—a clinician's ritual for nervous-system reset and better sleep.
posts.build_brief:
Format: vertical reel cover (1080×1920 portrait, optimised for Instagram Reels). 

Visual Structure (faithfully reuse the winner's composition):
- Full-frame photograph as the primary narrative anchor: shoot or source a moody, warm-toned bathroom scene with soft, low-level lighting (analogous to the competitor's candlelit ambiance). Think diffused warm light (soft gold/amber tones, not orange). The scene should evoke calm, parasympathetic activation — perhaps warm lamp light, dimmed fixtures, or gentle backlit elements. Ensure the bathroom and lighting feel clinical-warm, grounded, Australian-natural (avoid stock-wellness clichés).
- Overlay a semi-transparent, darkened layer (charcoal or soft black, ~70% opacity) across the lower two-thirds of the frame to ensure text contrast and maintain the moody atmosphere.
- ON TOP of this: centre-aligned text block, positioned centre-frame (vertically and horizontally dominant, matching the winner's dominance of whitespace/hierarchy). 

On-Image Text (exact copy — keep it SHORT to respect legibility floors):
Headline: "Dark showers." (one word: "showers" in green #39B54A italic, bold weight ~95px+ relative scale).
Supporting line (below headline, Satoshi medium, ~40-45px relative scale, 3–4 lines max): "You were today years old when you found out they might help your sleep. Here's why."

Brand lockup: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green #39B54A, Satoshi bold, ~30–32px relative scale, bottom-centre, subtle and unobtrusive).

Colour Palette (NEVER the competitor's orange/candlelight palette):
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (headline word, brand lockup).
- Secondary palette: Warm sage-grey (bathroom tones), soft cream underlay, charcoal text/overlays. This harmonises with green and avoids the competitor's warm-orange dominance while maintaining the calm, low-light mood.
- Avoid: bright orange, warm amber, pure black background. The overlay is charcoal; the background is the warm-lit bathroom (sage-grey undertones, cream-tinted shadows), creating a clinical-warm feel aligned with EHN's brand.

Layout Logic (match the winner's whitespace + scale + density):
- The image dominates (no border, edge-to-edge).
- Text is centred and BOLD — a clear, single focal point (as the competitor's text is — no clutter).
- Generous whitespace around the text block (the overlay ensures readability without shrinking copy).
- Overall feel: moody, calm, editorial, evidence-backed — not clichéd wellness.

Legibility Assertion (1080 canvas):
- Headline "Dark showers" ≈ 95px+ (bold, Satoshi).
- Supporting line ≈ 40–45px (medium, Satoshi).
- Brand line ≈ 30px (bold, Satoshi).
- NO text below 36px absolute minimum (hard floor for rendering).

Render all text in neutral tones (white or cream for headline/supporting, green for brand) against the charcoal overlay to ensure contrast and clinical clarity. The reel should feel calm, purposeful, and clinician-credible — a moment of pause before the swipe.

posts.design_recipe:
REUSABLE TEMPLATE — Topic-Agnostic Reel Cover (Sleep/Nervous-System Focus)

Format: Vertical reel cover (1080×1920 portrait, Instagram Reels optimised).

Visual Foundation:
- Full-frame photograph as primary narrative (moody, low-light scene aligned with topic — warm ambient lighting, clinical-natural aesthetic, no stock clichés).
- Semi-transparent charcoal overlay (~70% opacity) across lower two-thirds to ensure text contrast and maintain atmospheric depth.

Typography & Hierarchy:
- Headline: [ONE WORD IN GREEN #39B54A ITALIC] + supporting context (Satoshi bold, ~95px+ relative scale for the anchor word; supporting words ~60–70px relative scale for hierarchy).
- Supporting line: [2–4 short supporting phrases] (Satoshi medium, ~40–45px relative scale, centre-aligned, max 3–4 lines).
- Brand lockup: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi bold, ~30–32px relative scale, green #39B54A, bottom-centre, unobtrusive).

Colour Palette (Green-Anchored, Harmonised):
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (headline word, brand lockup — mandatory).
- Secondary palette: Warm sage-grey, soft cream, charcoal overlays — chosen to harmonise with green and suit the topic's mood. Palette flexibility: flex secondary colours per topic (e.g., muted teal for nervous-system calm, soft terracotta for grounding), but always ensure colours harmonise with green and avoid clashing tones (no bright purple, neon, or harsh reds).
- Background: The full-frame photograph (warm, ambient, clinical-natural tones); overlay is charcoal (never pure black).

Layout & Whitespace (Match the Winner's Dominance):
- Image dominates edge-to-edge (no border, no card frame).
- Text block centred vertically and horizontally — a single, clear focal point.
- Generous whitespace around text (the overlay provides contrast; copy is NOT shrunk).
- Feel: moody, calm, editorial, grounded — clinical-warm, never clichéd wellness.

Legibility Assertion (1080 canvas hard floors):
- Headline anchor word ≥95px (bold, Satoshi).
- Supporting line ≥40px (medium, Satoshi).
- Brand line ≥30px (bold, Satoshi).
- NO text renders below 36px absolute minimum.
- Rule: SHORTEN COPY, NEVER SHRINK FONT. If copy is long, cut words; do not reduce point size.

Text Rendering:
- Headline & supporting line: white or cream text against charcoal overlay (high contrast, clinical clarity).
- Brand lockup: green #39B54A (visual anchor, brand identity).

Overall Aesthetic:
Moody, purposeful, parasympathetic-calm — a moment of editorial pause. Clinical-credible, Australian-natural, evidence-backed. The green anchor ties every post to EHN brand identity while secondary palette harmonises and flexes per topic.
posts.caption:
Dark showers are one of my favourite gentle rituals to recommend to patients struggling with nervous-system overload, poor sleep, or that "wired but tired" feeling. Here's the thinking: reducing light exposure—especially blue light from bathroom mirrors and overhead globes—keeps your melatonin climbing as you wind down. A dimly lit shower (even just switching off the bright overhead and relying on a softer lamp or candlelight) signals to your nervous system that it's time to pause, rest, and prepare for sleep. It's simple, free, and it works with your body's natural rhythms rather than against them. If you're struggling with sleep quality or that restless, switched-on feeling at night, this might be worth a try. Have you experimented with dimmer lighting in your evening routine? We'd love to hear how you go—and if you'd like to explore what's driving your sleep troubles more deeply, we're here to help. Book an appointment to discuss your sleep and nervous-system health with us.

#DarkShowers #SleepHygiene #NervousSystemHealth #ParasympatheticCalm #SleepWellness #AdelaideWellness #FunctionalMedicine
posts.gmb_caption: Dark showers—dimmed lighting in your evening bathroom routine—can help your nervous system downregulate and support better sleep. It's a simple, free ritual many of our Eastwood patients have found helpful for calming that "wired but tired" feeling. If sleep troubles or nervous-system overload are affecting you, book an appointment to explore what might help.
